_readme: cot_radio_aprs v2.0 canonical iconset dictionary. Published here for Part 97 transparency — any licensed amateur operator can download this file and decode the 4-char icon codes used in v2.0 packets on ham radio frequencies. Additive-only within a major version; new icons get new IDs, existing IDs never change. Removing or renumbering requires a major-version bump and invalidates archived traffic.

D.name: Default ATAK
D.source: AndroidTacticalAssaultKit-CIV (https://github.com/deptofdefense/AndroidTacticalAssaultKit-CIV) — the canonical ATAK client iconset

F.name: FEMA Incident Management
F.source: FEMA-published incident-command iconset, widely distributed in the emergency-management community and bundled with many TAK deployments

_notes.id_ranges: IDs are loosely themed by hundred-blocks for human mnemonics: 000-049 Animals, 050-099 Transportation, 100-149 People, 150-199 Weapons, 200-249 Structures, 250-299 Hazards (in Default ATAK iconset D). Themes are advisory, not normative — receivers MUST treat IDs as opaque lookups.
_notes.populating: This initial v1.0 is a seed. Additional icons get appended as operators identify need; each addition bumps the version minor digit.
_notes.custom_iconsets: Operator-custom iconsets use codes X, Y, Z via a separate iconset_dict.user.json maintained out-of-band and synced across the operator's network. Publishing that user-level dict is required for Part 97 compliance when used on amateur frequencies.
